votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq functions, including the exact behavior of max_list which ignores the default argument for non-empty lists, and accurately translates the recursive list building into an iterative loop.

The Python code accurately translates the Coq functions and specification, correctly implementing the list maximum, prefix extraction, and rolling maximum logic.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq definitions. max_list computes the maximum of a list (returning default for empty lists), firstn returns the first n elements, rolling_max_aux builds a list of rolling maximums by iterating from 1 to idx, and rolling_max_spec checks that each position i contains the maximum of the first i+1 elements. The Python implementations match the Coq behavior on all concrete inputs.

Python functions implement the same prefix-max logic as the Coq definitions: max_list ignores the default for non-empty lists, firstn via slicing matches Coq’s firstn, rolling_max_aux builds prefix maxima in order, rolling_max uses full length, and rolling_max_spec checks the same property.
